Petition For Request To Approve And Allow Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Aug-28-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Cristina Perez-Abelson appeared by telephone for Carmen Mercado and Ivania Maginniss (Trustees).
Kevin Urbatsch appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it reviewed the supplement to the accounting, that the payment of fees without a Court order was completely inappropriate and that it seems everyone's position is that it was covered in the last accounting. The Court also stated that it had trouble squaring with the other information in the time records, that there was conversation between Counsel about the fees and questions the payment to the Quigley Quarterly Trust. In addition, the Court stated that it does not see any expenditures for Ms. Mercado, no outings or clothing, and questions what is being done to benefit Anna Mercado and enrich her life. Ms. Abelson stated that they spent a lot of time downloading and obtaining the prior accounting, that she did not check that item and that it was a huge oversight on her part. Mr. Urbatsch stated that he is well aware that he is not to receive fees without a Court order, that it was a complete mistake on his part, that the Trustee immediately sent a check and that when his staff received the check, they applied it to the outstanding invoice. Mr. Urbatsch also stated that there was nothing in place at that time to check that, that they now have a procedure in place to verify if they are Court approved fees and that he is happy to refund the fees to the trust. Ms. Abelson stated that they now have a protocol in place at her office as well. Mr. Urbatsch confirmed that the payment to Quigley Quarterly Trust is for Christine's services and that she does the accounting and the schedules for the accounting. Ms. Abelson stated that Ms. Mercado often pays for things out of pocket and asks for reimbursement, but she hadn't been doing a good job at keeping track of her receipts and that this has been implemented by giving Carmen Mercado a stack of pre-paid postage envelopes so when she purchases items, she can place the receipts in the envelope and send them to her. The Court asked Ms. Abelson to inform the Co-Trustees that it is really their duty to do this, that they should get help and that if they can't do it, they should consider if they should be the trustees at all. The Court also stated that it wants to see this trust benefit Anna, that the next accounting will be a two-year accounting, that it will not be the same thing and that they should have receipts. Mr. Urbatsch stated that he filed a supplements addressing the issues raised by the Examiner, that he withdrew some of his requests and voluntarily reduced some. The Fifth Account and Report of Trustees, and the Petition for Payment of Attorney Fees are under submission. Status on filing of the sixth account is set for November 5, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting is to be filed by September 28, 2020 or an appearance is required to explain why it has not been filed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
